notes: Kiro /rewind forks: the original session retains turns one and two on disk, a new rewind-born session opens at the selected one checkpoint and receives three. The daemon emits transcript_removed for the original at fork time even though its JSONL survives; this fixture preserves both transcripts and asserts the new-session arm of the scenario.

text: Kiro process pre-session appears before its JSONL is written
Kiro process binds to the pre-session
Original Kiro JSONL session replaces the pre-session
Kiro process binds to the original JSONL session
First prompt starts on the original session
Second prompt starts on the original session
Confirming /rewind creates a distinct ready session from the selected checkpoint
The daemon removes the original session row at fork time although the original JSONL remains on disk
Post-rewind prompt starts on the new session
Post-rewind turn completes on the new session
